rules.description: Produce the most natural-sounding translation capturing RingCentral content's intended meaning for a wide Spanish-speaking audience
rules.notes: Avoid literal transposition of English syntax; sentences may need to be rewritten with adapted syntax

rules.description: Use clear and precise style that should not read like a translation
rules.notes: Balance faithfulness to source and readability in target language

rules.description: Avoid heaviness, ambiguities, repetitions and Anglicisms
rules.notes: Also avoid enriching the text excessively

rules.description: Translate buttons as verbs, generally using infinitive form
rules.notes: Be concise, avoid using articles or prepositions

rules.description: Pay attention to character/space limitations in buttons
rules.example_correct: Vaya a Página de perfiles y haga clic en Añadir categorías
rules.example_incorrect: Vaya a la página de perfiles y haga clic en el botón de añadir categorías
rules.notes: Depending on project-specific requirements

rules.description: Use direct imperative forms in button instructions
rules.example_correct: Pulse OK para guardar los cambios
rules.example_incorrect: Recuerde pulsar el botón OK para guardar todos los cambios

rules.description: Simplify button action descriptions
rules.example_incorrect: Si desea revisar el documento, tendrá que descargarlo

rules.section: Software Localization - Names of RingCentral Products
rules.description: Check reference material and linguistic instructions before translating product/application names
rules.notes: Many product/application names are trademarked and not localizable

rules.description: Replace English examples with Spanish equivalents when needed
rules.notes: Particularly important for wildcard operators and similar technical explanations

rules.description: Be precise, clear, consistent and natural
rules.notes: Rephrase unclear sentences and rework sentences that sound like translations

rules.description: Break up long English sentences into smaller friendlier parts for better understanding
Use accurate and concise style with no ambiguities or mistranslations
Maintain clear and coherent style that target reader can understand easily
Be objective and neutral, avoid colloquial or slang words, be politically correct
rules.notes: Do not insert your own opinion

rules.description: Ensure content and numbers are free of mistakes
Use idiomatically correct verbs, metaphors, expressions so text doesn't sound like translation
Be complete - do not leave out content (words, phrases, whole paragraphs)
Keep an eye on the target reader and use appropriate tone
Translate internal cross-references consistently throughout translation and all files
rules.notes: References to chapters, sections, headings or titles within the file or document being translated

rules.description: For external cross-references, check if text has been localized before and match relevant translations
rules.notes: References to titles of other documents not being translated or already translated in previous projects

rules.description: If external document has not been translated before, leave as in source language
rules.notes: Contact Project Management team or Language Lead if unable to retrieve translation information

rules.description: Use formal style to address user (usted) but avoid overusing 'Please'
rules.example_incorrect: Por favor, introduce tu nombre de usuario.
rules.notes: Be more direct and use imperative form where appropriate

rules.description: Spanish gives priority to masculine forms unless differently needed based on specific context
rules.example_correct: Los usuarios pueden contactar con la asistencia al cliente cuando necesiten ayuda.
rules.example_incorrect: Los usuarios y las usuarias pueden contactar con la asistencia al cliente cuando necesiten ayuda.

rules.description: Avoid gendered adjectives whenever possible
Avoid passive voice when possible, use active form or reflexive passive voice (se) to improve readability
rules.example_correct: Se han facilitado todos los detalles sobre este tema.
rules.example_incorrect: Todos los detalles sobre este tema han sido facilitados.
rules.notes: Passive voice only advised in legal and official documents (privacy policies, contracts, NDAs, etc.)

rules.description: Use active voice instead of passive voice in technical reports
rules.example_correct: En este informe técnico aparecen algunas funciones nuevas.
rules.example_incorrect: En este informe técnico son detalladas algunas funciones nuevas.

rules.description: Don't transfer extensive use of English possessive adjectives to Spanish
rules.example_correct: Puede editar los archivos desde un ordenador o desde su teléfono móvil.
rules.example_incorrect: Puede editar sus archivos desde su ordenador o desde su teléfono móvil.
rules.notes: Simple articles can often replace possessive adjectives; 'our' sometimes refers to third person

rules.description: Use articles according to standard Spanish grammar rules
Translate -ing form based on context (verb, noun, infinitive clause, etc.) but avoid gerund when describing action after/consequence of another or when acting like adjective
rules.example_correct: Nuestros controladores disponen de una memoria que protege la integridad de sus datos.
rules.example_incorrect: Nuestros controladores disponen de una memoria protegiendo la integridad de sus datos.

rules.description: Use conditional structures instead of gerunds for sequential actions
rules.example_correct: Si accede al sitio web, acepta los términos y condiciones.
rules.example_incorrect: Accediendo al sitio web, acepta los términos y condiciones.

rules.description: Never translate gerunds in English headings and titles as gerunds - use noun form or verbal forms consistently
Use present tense instead of gerund in product introductions
Check context and render meaning as clearly as possible, avoid additional words used in English
rules.example_incorrect: Introduzca su nombre, su dirección de correo electrónico y su fecha de nacimiento.

rules.description: Simplify verbose English expressions
rules.example_correct: Ahora puede acceder a todo el contenido.
rules.example_incorrect: Esto significa que a partir de ahora puede acceder a todo el contenido.

rules.description: Use pronouns or other methods to avoid repetitions that are acceptable in English but not in Spanish
rules.example_correct: Para eliminar un archivo de su ordenador, selecciónelo en el panel derecho y haga clic en Suprimir.
rules.example_incorrect: Para eliminar un archivo de su ordenador, seleccione el archivo en el panel derecho y haga clic en Suprimir.

rules.description: Avoid literal translations of false friends
rules.notes: For 'Abort'

rules.description: Translate 'Actually' correctly
Translate 'Assess' correctly
Translate 'Assume' correctly
Translate 'Billion' correctly
Translate 'Consistent' correctly
Translate 'Eventual' correctly
Translate 'Fabric' correctly
Translate 'Facility' correctly
Translate 'Resume' correctly
Translate 'Support' correctly
Follow Spanish capitalization rules which differ from English
Capitalize proper names, product names, geographical names
Capitalize first word after period, exclamation mark, question mark, ellipsis when sentence is different or repetition
Capitalize after colon only when sentence is quote, explanatory sentence (enumerations), or note/notice/advertisement/warning
Use lowercase for nationalities, languages, religions, days of week, months, season names, currencies, job titles
Use lowercase for months
rules.example_correct: Estará listo a partir de julio.
rules.example_incorrect: Estará listo a partir de Julio.

rules.description: Use lowercase for currencies
Use lowercase for languages
Use lowercase for nationalities
In titles, headings, UI options, only capitalize first word of string
Capitalize only first word in terms and conditions titles
Capitalize only first word in UI elements
Retain capitalization in legal texts where it conveys specific meaning
Preserve capitalization of 'Software' in legal contexts
rules.example_correct: Instalar y usar una copia del Software
rules.example_incorrect: Instalar y usar una copia del software

rules.description: Preserve all uppercase formatting in paragraphs as these may have legal implications
rules.notes: Sometimes whole paragraphs are written with all upper case

rules.description: Use correct ASCII codes for Spanish quotation marks
rules.notes: Alt+0147 for ", Alt+0148 for ", Alt+0171 for «, Alt+0187 for »

rules.description: Run spell-checker when translation is completed
Separate brand name tags from preceding or following text with a space
No space before any punctuation sign in Spanish
Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces in Spanish
rules.notes: English documents often include double spaces after full stops

rules.description: Use non-breaking spaces to prevent separation of names, numbers, software names and versions at end of line
rules.notes: Use Ctrl + Shift + Space in MS Word or Alt+0160

rules.description: Use comma when natural pause is heard
rules.notes: Use of comma is quite subjective

rules.description: In enumeration, separate each element with comma; last element preceded by conjunction without comma
Do not separate subject from predicate with comma
Vocative particle must be preceded and/or followed by comma
Use period to end sentence, paragraph or abbreviated word
When sentence is between brackets or quotes, place period outside
Do not use full stops after headings, titles, table columns, captions unless full sentence
Do not use full stops if sentence ends with abbreviation, interrogation/exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon
Avoid using colons as English makes wider use of them; prefer commas where possible
Use colon to introduce enumerations, explanations, notices, advertisements, warnings
Use uppercase after headings like Advertencia, Precaución, Peligro, Nota when statement is full sentence
Use semi-colons less than in English; mainly to separate enumeration items when commas would be confusing
rules.notes: As rule, avoid semi-colons and start new sentence instead

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Exclamation mark
rules.description: Exclamation marks not as common in Spanish as English; avoid unless necessary
rules.notes: Emphasize main unit of interest rather than whole sentence

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Exclamation mark
rules.description: When exclamation marks appear in groups (!!!), keep only one

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: Use hyphen (-) to join two parts of compound term

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: Use en dash (-) as minus sign or in number ranges; do not insert space between number and en dash

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: Use em dash (—) to separate element within sentence; always close with final em dash even before period
rules.notes: Always spaces around it but not inside; avoid when can be replaced by brackets

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Hyphen/En dash/Em dash
rules.description: Replace single em dash in sentence with appropriate punctuation for context
rules.example_correct: Los miembros del jurado dieron un veredicto: culpable.
rules.example_incorrect: The jurors reached a unanimous verdict⁠ — guilty. >> Los miembros del jurado dieron un veredicto⁠ — culpable.
rules.notes: Like colon or comma

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Round and square brackets
rules.description: Use round brackets commonly; square brackets only when round brackets already appear in sentence
rules.notes: Square brackets often used to define tags

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Quotation marks
rules.description: NEVER use straight single quote for variables

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Quotation marks
rules.description: Use quotation marks for quotes from other sources or document titles; avoid single quotes unless software requirements force their use

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Quotation marks
rules.description: Place punctuation outside quotes

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Quotation marks
rules.description: Use angular quotation marks («») in Spanish

rules.section: Punctuation - Use of punctuation - Quotation marks
rules.description: To enclose quotation within quotation, use curly quotation marks ("")

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists - Example 1
rules.description: If bulleted items are complete sentences with conjugated verb, begin with capital and end with period
rules.example_correct:
• Inicie sesión y diríjase a la página de perfil.
• Haga clic en Preferencias y seleccione la opción Idioma.

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists - Example 2
rules.description: If bulleted items are phrases or single words, do not follow with period
rules.example_correct:
• Símbolos numéricos
• Símbolos y caracteres alfanuméricos
• Cadenas de valores

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists - Example 3
rules.description: If list items depend syntactically on introductory sentence, start with lowercase, end with semicolon except last element ending with period
rules.notes: When there's conjunction in penultimate element, semicolon goes before it

rules.description: Meta descriptions have maximum of 155 characters including spaces and punctuation
rules.notes: Should not be shorter than 50 characters or longer than 155 characters

rules.description: Meta titles have maximum of 55 characters including spaces and punctuation
Follow title case capitalization/formatting rules for meta titles
Separate 'RingCentral' from rest of meta title with horizontal bar
Use en dash (–) to separate keywords/keyword phrases
Avoid abbreviations whenever possible but may need to abbreviate in UI options
rules.notes: Preferably use standardized abbreviations

rules.description: Use standard abbreviation for Código
Use standard abbreviation for Departamento
Use standard abbreviation for Estados Unidos
Use standard abbreviation for Etcétera
Use standard abbreviation for Gran Bretaña
Use standard abbreviation for Máximo
Use standard abbreviation for Mínimo
Use standard abbreviation for Número
Use standard abbreviation for Página
Use standard abbreviation for Por ejemplo
Use standard abbreviation for Teléfono
Use standard abbreviation for Unión Europea
Translation of acronyms depends on RingCentral project requirements and should be investigated for localizability
Acronyms should not have plural endings
If acronym translation is possible, locate it before English expansion
rules.example_correct: CSV (valores separados por comas, Comma Separated Values)

rules.description: If acronym translation is not possible, leave expansion in parenthesis
If expansion comes before acronym and translation is possible, add English expansion in parentheses after acronym
rules.example_correct: Revoluciones por minuto (RPM, revolution per minute)

rules.description: If expansion comes before acronym and translation is not possible, leave expansion and acronym in parentheses
If acronym is not followed by expansion, use acronym without expansion
Write numbers in full when used as nouns
Write numbers in full when at beginning of sentence
Write numbers in full when appearing alone and representing simple quantities, usually up to 9
Write numbers in full in expressions like 'in the 80s'
Write dates, large numbers, ages, document references as numbers
Replace decimal point with comma
Replace thousand separator with non-breaking space when number has 5 digits or more
Use non-breaking space as thousand separator for numbers with 5+ digits
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms
Separate units of measure from preceding number by non-breaking space
Convert imperial units to metric based on project requirements
rules.notes: Monitor sizes always expressed in inches as exception

rules.description: Write percentages in figures followed by % symbol with no blank space
rules.notes: Spanish grammar recommends space but can cause confusion with many numbers

rules.description: Adapt date and time formats to Spanish standards
Use day-month format, not month-day
Use DD/MM/YYYY format for short dates
rules.notes: Can be separated by hyphen (-) or slash

rules.description: Use dddd de mmmmm de yyyy format for long dates
Use hh:mm:ss format for time with 24-hour clock
Convert time ranges to 24-hour format
Use Spanish date format for full dates
Use Spanish template with es_ES locale
rules.notes: For RC Spanish US/CA/UK/EU/AU brand with Spanish language display name 'Español'

rules.description: Use 'Español' as template name for Spanish fax cover
Use layout with Left: To | Right: From for Spanish fax covers
Place currency symbols after numeral preceded by non-breaking space with two decimal figures
Use Canadian dollar with numerals: 100 $. Add (CAD) in parentheses if needed for clarity
Use European currency with numerals without EUR: 100 €
Use Great Britain pound with numerals without GBP: 100 £
Use US dollar with numerals: 100 $. Add (USD) in parentheses if needed for clarity
Translate 'About' as 'Acerca de, sobre, alrededor de'
Translate 'as shown in figure xx' as 'tal como aparece en la imagen xx'
rules.example_correct: tal como aparece en la imagen xx

rules.description: Translate 'For more information on xx, see xxx.' as 'Para obtener más información sobre xx, consulte xxx.'
rules.example_correct: Para obtener más información sobre xx, consulte xxx.
